name: Security Pin False-Set Feedback Decision Theory Grammar
mechanism: Security Pin False-Set Feedback in lock-picking / security engineering operates through the interaction of spool or serrated security pin under tension as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through geometry of security pin (spool, mushroom, serrated) and applied torque, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—counter-rotation feedback distinguishes standard pins from security pins—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by picker's ability to decode the false set and adjust tension. This process is bounded by number of security pins, torque sensitivity, tool geometry, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to decision theory, the mechanism from lock-picking / security engineering suggests that the core units of decision theory undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (geometry of security pin (spool, mushroom, serrated) and applied torque) maps onto the generative processes in decision theory, while the selection pressure (counter-rotation feedback distinguishes standard pins from security pins) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that decision theory systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in lock-picking / security engineering, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (number of security pins, torque sensitivity, tool geometry) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in decision theory designed using lock-picking / security engineering structural principles will show 22% lower error rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=120 cases over a 12-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Satisficing Geomorphology Grammar
mechanism: Satisficing (Simon) in decision theory operates through the interaction of decision-maker searching alternatives as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through aspiration level and search order, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—first option meeting threshold accepted; optimization abandoned—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by decision speed vs optimality tradeoff under bounded rationality. This process is bounded by cognitive limits, search cost, time pressure, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to geomorphology, the mechanism from decision theory suggests that the core units of geomorphology undergo an analogous evolutionary process. This cross-domain mapping predicts that geomorphology systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in decision theory, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (cognitive limits, search cost, time pressure) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in geomorphology designed using decision theory structural principles will show 20% higher performance scores compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=40 cases over a 12-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Two-Process Model of Sleep Regulation Agroecology Grammar
mechanism: Two-Process Model of Sleep Regulation (Borbely) in sleep science operates through the interaction of sleep pressure / circadian phase as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through homeostatic sleep pressure (Process S) and circadian drive (Process C), creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—sleep onset when S exceeds upper threshold; wake when it falls below lower—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by sleep timing and duration optimized. This process is bounded by circadian period ~24h, ultradian oscillation of REM/NREM within sleep, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to agroecology, the mechanism from sleep science suggests that the core units of agroecology undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (homeostatic sleep pressure (Process S) and circadian drive (Process C)) maps onto the generative processes in agroecology, while the selection pressure (sleep onset when S exceeds upper threshold; wake when it falls below lower) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that agroecology systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in sleep science, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (circadian period ~24h, ultradian oscillation of REM/NREM within sleep) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in agroecology designed using sleep science structural principles will show 35% improvement in resilience measures compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=120 cases over a 18-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Marangoni Drainage Stabilization Forensic Science Topology
mechanism: Marangoni Drainage Stabilization in soap bubble physics operates through the interaction of surfactant concentration gradient along a draining film as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through surfactant molecular weight, CMC, and adsorption kinetics alter the strength of the Marangoni counterflow, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—surfactant systems where Marangoni stress opposes gravitational thinning produce longer-lived films—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by film lifetime before rupture (seconds to minutes for demonstration foams). This process is bounded by evaporation rate in dry air, surfactant desorption at extreme thinning, mechanical perturbation, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

This cross-domain mapping predicts that forensic science systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in soap bubble physics, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (evaporation rate in dry air, surfactant desorption at extreme thinning, mechanical perturbation) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in forensic science designed using soap bubble physics structural principles will show 12% reduction in resource waste compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=60 cases over a 24-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Lead Came Structural Flex Fatigue Cycle Swarm Intelligence Strategy
mechanism: Lead Came Structural Flex Fatigue Cycle in stained glass operates through the interaction of single lead came joint between two glass pieces as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through came profile (H vs. U), alloy hardness, solder joint size, panel orientation (vertical vs. angled), wind load cycling, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—thermal expansion and wind pressure flex the came; softer lead absorbs more cycles but sags under gravity; harder alloys resist sag but crack at solder joints sooner—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by panel maintains structural integrity and weather-seal for 50+ years in its installed orientation. This process is bounded by does not cover copper foil technique (Tiffany method) which has entirely different stress distribution, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to swarm intelligence, the mechanism from stained glass suggests that the core units of swarm intelligence undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (came profile (H vs. U), alloy hardness, solder joint size, panel orientation (vertical vs. angled), wind load cycling) maps onto the generative processes in swarm intelligence, while the selection pressure (thermal expansion and wind pressure flex the came; softer lead absorbs more cycles but sags under gravity; harder alloys resist sag but crack at solder joints sooner) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. The boundary conditions (does not cover copper foil technique (Tiffany method) which has entirely different stress distribution) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in swarm intelligence designed using stained glass structural principles will show 28% increase in output quality compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=40 cases over a 6-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Submarine Cable Signal Dispersion Geomorphology Dynamics
